programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark suspects an eminent senator of killing his own daughter, whose behaviour was jeopardising his re-election campaign. Determined to discover the truth, he calls in Amanda to ask her advice. Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell star

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan and the District Attorney visit a school to investigate a drive-by shooting that is not all it seems. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: A blind girl with a vicious disposition almost costs Kimble his freedom. Guest starring Tuesday Weld

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Delko is rushed to hospital with potentially fatal gunshot wounds, and Horatio waits anxiously as medics try to revive him, while the rest of the team searches for his assailant. Calleigh and Ryan process the crime scene, and question a security guard who provides them with what appears to be vital information - until further evidence sheds new light on the case

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when a mysterious, fast-spreading illness strikes children from military families. While Gibbs tries to determine the cause, Abby and Jimmy partner with the Naval Medical Research Centre in the hope of delivering a cure before the holidays. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate when former marine Asher Ross is killed by an explosive device hidden in his mobile phone. Because of the sophisticated nature of the bomb, suspicion falls on the members of the ordnance disposal unit Ross was part of in Iraq, but further enquiries reveal that they are being targeted as well

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: The unconscious patient in a hospital in Decatur, Illinois, is really Richard Kimble, a victim of amnesia who does not remember he is innocent

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A group of citizens frustrated by the judicial system take the law into their own hands and set about killing all the bad guys in town

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of two dishonourably discharged officers are found on board a boat belonging to Gibbs, and inquiries cast suspicion on his mentor, retired agent Mike Franks. The case proves to have wider implications - and the team finds itself battling to prevent an international confrontation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. Henri Lubatti guest stars

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella try to establish why two women have made conflicting claims to be the wife of a murder victim, and make a surprising discovery about the circumstances of their relationships. Meanwhile, Danny and Lindsay investigate the killing of a woman found near a dock, tangled up in seaweed and dressed as a mermaid. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter's parents and the team visit Megan in hospital as her health deteriorates, but end up quarantined when a deadly fungus outbreak threatens to infect everyone

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Members of the team go undercover at university to find a hacker putting the world economy at risk. Walter tries to prove he was not to blame for Ray's partner's death

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy recruitment officer is murdered at a high school and the agents discover he had recently begun a relationship with a married teacher whose wife held a grudge against him. They also find out a student's father was vehemently opposed to his joining the military, and receive help from an unexpected quarter - Ducky's esteemed predecessor, Dr Magnus. Crime drama, guest starring comedian Bob Newhart

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A former serviceman who had been having an affair is beaten to death in his own back garden, and the agents are forced to consider the possibility that his wife - a US Marine and martial arts expert - is the killer. Meanwhile, McGee becomes the subject of an embarrassing mix-up when an inflatable doll apparently purchased on his credit card is delivered. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are forced to undergo psychological evaluations conducted by Dr Rachel Cranston (guest star Wendy Makkena) while investigating the death of a submarine's commanding officer. Suspicion falls on a journalist seen arguing with the man before his demise, and Tony discovers a link between the psychotherapist shadowing the agents and Ziva's predecessor Kate Todd. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Gibbs and the team are given the task of protecting the politician's daughter while she studies in America, but fail to stop her being kidnapped and ransomed in a bid to prevent her father signing an important treaty. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Medium

programme.title: Medium

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison is puzzled when she begins to see strange symbols, and is informed they might be a result of her recent brain surgery. Supernatural drama, starring Patricia Arquette

programme.desc: Part one of two. A paranoid schizophrenic is the main suspect in the case of a prowler who attacked a woman and then stabbed Reg, who answered the call for help. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Part one of two. Connie and Joseph scheme to perform Elaine's operation despite Ric's insistence they deal only with emergency cases, and Donna unwittingly puts Mia in harm's way

programme.title: Casualty

Will is entertaining another woman when he learns from Harry about Selena's stabbing, and Jamie arrives to tell Maggie he can't cope on his own at home with Stan

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Schizophrenic Michael Simms threatens Shelley Cooper with a knife and when the woman is later killed, Neil orders CO19 to shoot him, assuming Simms is the culprit. Meanwhile, back at the restaurant, Sally is harassed by a finicky diner. Richard Griffiths stars

programme.title: Lovejoy

programme.desc: Lionel is delighted to be reunited with Veronica, leading him to treat his employees much kinder than before - but nothing lasts forever. Guest starring Pippa Haywood (Green Wing)

programme.desc: Foggy, Clegg and Compo try to make Sid appear dashing in Ivy's eyes, convincing Weasley to use him in his latest experiment. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, John Comer and Gordon Wharmby

programme.desc: Foggy persuades Clegg and Compo that starting a dog-walking business will be no work at all since they are already going on daily countryside rambles. However, the experience proves to be more of a workout than the trio envisage. Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An old friend of Dalziel arouses suspicion by claiming a series of apparently accidental deaths may have a more sinister explanation. Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star, with Keith Barron

programme.desc: The team investigates the mystery of a missing scientist, after the man's wife receives a series of emails from someone claiming to know what happened to him. The physics professor was last seen in 2004, on a train to Paris for a conference, and when he disappeared he was working on a potentially lucrative cold fusion project that had attracted attention from many large organisations. However, as the detectives dig deeper, a shadowy Whitehall figure warns Sandra's boss Strickland they should drop the case

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An anonymous e-mail leads the squad to wasteground in east London, where the remains of a right-wing political activist are found. programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the killing of notorious biker-gang leader Eddie Chapman when his son's girlfriend brings them new information about his death. Meanwhile, Sandra is forced to reassess her position at Ucos when a former colleague suggests she is wasting her career. Starring Amanda Redman, with her real-life daughter Emily Glenister in a supporting role

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When the body of salesman Chris Dearden is found in a disused tunnel, investigations reveal he had been reported missing by his wife Lucy in 1996. However, the victim's son refuses to believe the body is his father's

Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Michael Culver, John Hallam and Anna Friel

programme.desc: Election fever grips Skelthwaite, but Simon finds his opponent more formidable and devious than the weedy kid he used to bully at school. Meanwhile, Beth uncovers Anita's beauty secrets and plans to make things difficult for estranged husband Stewart

Drama, starring Leslie Ash, Lesley Dunlop, Tony Haygarth and Lee Warburton

However, they find themselves in the right place at the right time when the body of a wine critic is discovered on the premises. Murder mystery, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The amateur sleuths are called to a monastery to restore a herb garden in time for a royal visit - but their pruning and planting is disrupted by the discovery of a corpse. As they investigate the death, the tight-knit community closes in around them. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo help reorganise the seed museum owned by the descendants of famous botanist Edwin Pargeter - but it is not long before a man is found dead in the family's garden. In an effort to trace his killer, Rosemary involves colleagues from the Royal Horticultural Society at Kew Gardens, and soon the race is on to identify a batch of mysterious seeds before it falls into the wrong hands. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Shepherd and his team are confronted with the sight of a Santa Claus with a slit throat. The victim is actually much-loved local mayor Evan Whitestone, who had dressed as Santa every year for the Christmas parade. Suspicion soon turns to the four rivals for his mayoral chains in a forthcoming election. Neill Rea and Fern Sutherland star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest investigates when the new wife of Sir Raleigh is found dead in a locked room after seeing an Egyptian mummy. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Max and Fenton investigate a spate of counterfeit notes, and wonder if it is a coincidence that pickpocket Brody suddenly seems well-off. Meanwhile, Gina is put forward for a risky assignment, and the outcome of the boxing match leaves everyone reeling

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: An informant is due to give evidence against his former boss, one of Glasgow's most powerful gang leaders - but police fail to get the witness to court. Drama, with Mark McManus, Blythe Duff and James MacPherson. Co-star Gray O'Brien makes one of six appearances in the series, years before playing Tony Gordon in Coronation Street. More recently he popped up in The Loch, and Shakespeare & Hathaway: Private Investigators. Director Richard Holthouse went on to helm several episodes of Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Fraser calls off his goons and agrees to let the Magnificent Seven stay in the manor house rent-free, while Oz encounters a lady who is keen to make the most of his company. Morag Hood guest stars, with Jimmy Nail

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Work at Thornley Manor is over and the lads take a break before leaving for Spain. Oz uses the opportunity to visit home and hits on the barmy idea of kidnapping his son, determined to prevent him from being taken overseas by his wife Marjorie - but makes the mistake of roping Wayne in to help

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The monks cause a storm of resentment by launching an expedition to a Welsh village to recover the bones of St Winifred and return them to the abbey. As if that does not make them unpopular enough, a local landowner dies in mysterious circumstances and it is up to Brother Cadfael to clear his colleagues of murder and ensure they get out of the country alive. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Michael Culver, John Hallam and Anna Friel

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a spice box, two Chinese statues, a funfair horse, a 1900s cabinet and a dummy called Steve. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd bring viewers the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With the evidence stacked against him, Phil is running out of options - and things only get worse when Max starts piling on the pressure. Linda is furious after realising that Jack told his brother about Mick's abuse. Martin asks Kush for advice after Iqra's revelation about Ruby, and Jean and Sharon have some fun at Suki's expense

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as the second group of quarter-finalists give some scruffy bichons frisés their trademark white cloud look, and try to identify a cross-breed's two parents. In the final challenge, they must trim their dogs' coats to create a homage to a place close to their hearts, before showing off their creations on the Dogwalk. But who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor take through to next week?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A man confesses to murdering his boss, despite being unable to remember what happened. Convinced that things are not as simple as they seem, Neville determines to uncover the truth and unmask the real killer. Detective drama, starring Ralf Little

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate. On the panel are MPs Mark Harper, Nadia Whittome and Layla Moran, alongside Linda Bauld, Professor of Public Health at the University of Edinburgh, and Peter Borg-Neal, the CEO of Oakman Inns

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A father helps his daughter out buying and transforming a maisonette in Ewell, Surrey, for her to live in, and in Derby two friends hope to create smart accommodation for students. Plus, Martin Roberts and the team appraise a shop premises in Kent

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Beth Hood wants to start a new life for her family down under, but her husband Ben is not convinced, so they decide to spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A spate of burglaries at Christmas leads to a PCSO and his colleague being attacked, as they track down and try to arrest the thief. A West Midlands police officer returns to work after an assault, only to be attacked again just weeks later

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper, before seeing items go under the hammer with auctioneer Charles Hanson. Natasha also looks at the history of buying gifts for special occasions and finds that antiques fairs can the best place to buy that special wedding or christening present.

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Ines sends detectives Max and Miranda to the home of famous German reality TV host and record producer Jurgen Kuhl to investigate a report that equipment is being stolen. Max finds he has unwittingly proposed to his long-term girlfriend Carmen, while Miranda is also offered a proposal - one she'd be mad to refuse

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by fulfilling a lifetime's ambition to visit Japan, the home of their favourite food. The duo sample sushi at Tokyo's famous fish market and have the privilege of spending time in a sumo stable, where they take part in a wrestling bout

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley goes on a house-hunting mission to help a couple with a £525,000 budget find a country home in north Cornwall to retire to. Along the way, she visits the annual Royal Cornwall Show, which has showcased the county's heritage for over 220 years. While there, Ginny samples the traditional nettle-covered yarg cheese and sees if a very rare breed of sheep manages to win a best-in-class rosette

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea shows a crafty make to hang bathroom towels on, while Jay Blades builds an under-bed storage solution. Plus, Monty Don gives us the lowdown on the best hedge trimming tools, a dated armchair is given an outrageous upcycle, and Alan Titchmarsh shares his secrets on how to build a pond

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on a fairground dodgem, two figurines, a pair of ornate candlesticks, a vintage garden sprayer and a rescued mannequin

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd round off the week with a lively mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby, former Everton midfielder Leon Osman, ex-Great Britain swimmer Fran Halsall and Olympic 4x400m bronze medallist Anyika Onuora join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack steps in to try to diffuse the situation between Max and Mick, but when he realises what his brother has done, his blood runs cold. Meanwhile, Max tells Linda that they cannot move on if Max is around. Elsewhere, Stacey has a plan to prove that Ruby is lying and tells Kush to meet her at the club

programme.title: Stan & Ollie (2018)
programme.desc: Biography of Laurel and Hardy, focusing on the events of 1953 as the comedy duo try to re-ignite their film careers by embarking on a gruelling tour of British variety halls. As they set out on their demanding schedule, with their wives back home in the US, Stan is distracted by his attempts to pin down the producer of their proposed comeback film and Ollie tries to heal a rift caused by a perceived professional betrayal. The build-up of pressure starts to tarnish their precious partnership. Steve Coogan and John C Reilly star

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by guests Oscar-nominated British star Rosamund Pike, who discusses her new comedy thriller I Care a Lot. Acerbic chef Gordon Ramsay chats about his new BBC quiz show Bank Balance, while Welsh rugby union great Gareth Thomas reflects on his honest and challenging new book, Stronger. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville talks about playing Roald Dahl in To Olivia, while comedian and presenter Judi Love also guests. Plus, Ella Henderson and Tom Grennan perform Let's Go Home Together

programme.title: Witness (1985)
programme.desc: A Philadelphia cop is assigned to protect an Amish boy who was the only witness to a murder. Discovering the killers are rogue policemen willing to go to any lengths to cover up their crime, he is forced to go into hiding within the Amish community, experiencing a clash of cultures and unexpected feelings for the boy's widowed mother. Thriller, directed by Peter Weir, with Harrison Ford, Kelly McGillis, Lukas Haas and Danny Glover

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Yotam Ottolenghi, and special guests Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt. Plus, Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton and Arsenal v Manchester City

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the meeting in Torun, Poland, including the women's 60m, 3,000m and 800m, and men's 60m hurdles, pole vault and 1500m. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from the women's singles final at Melbourne Park in Australia, which was won by Sofia Kenin last year

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Retired couple David and Joyce's back garden in their home outside Gloucester looks more like an allotment than a garden. David is a dedicated vegetable grower and for Joyce, that means there's nowhere shady or pretty to sit and enjoy the garden. Meanwhile, David's increasing back and knee problems mean he is struggling to keep up its maintenance. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set out to make the garden both prettier and more user friendly, all within a £3,000 budget

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Impressionist Jon Culshaw, Richie Anderson from BBC Radio 2, broadcaster Rachel Johnson and actor Denis Lawson answer questions on Doctor Who, The Saturdays, The Laconia incident and Miles Davis

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, England goalkeepers David James and Siobhan Chamberlain take on rugby legend James Haskell and fitness coach Chloe Madeley

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees footballing legend John Barnes and football pundit Chris Kamara teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Peter Purves, Lesley Judd, Jane Tucker, Freddy Marks, Dave Benson Phillips, Mike Read, Kirsten O'Brien and Terrence Hardiman

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha and Ethan have kept their distance since Fenisha had the baby, but when their son is rushed into hospital, Fenisha realises that Ethan will be there to support her. Meanwhile, Connie is shocked by Grace's behaviour, and Rosa encourages Ollie to make a move. Starring George Rainsford, Olivia D'Lima and Amanda Mealing

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton is attacked while on remand in prison awaiting trial and tells Fleming she had a relationship with a high-ranking officer - who may have reason to want her framed. After AC-12 gains access to the files on the protected witness, the evidence suggests a possible police conspiracy and the cohesion of the team is further threatened by the arrival of a former adversary. Crime drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Southampton v Chelsea. Everton were looking for their first win away to their neighbours since 1999 and will have drawn confidence from some recent home defeats for the Reds. Southampton were looking to get back on track after a humiliating 9-0 defeat at the hands of Manchester United 17 days ago, while the Blues were hoping to move up the table under new manager Thomas Tuchel. Plus, two crucial matches in the battle to avoid relegation - Burnley v West Bromwich Albion and Fulham v Sheffield United

programme.title: Stronger (2017)
programme.desc: A man's life changes radically after he is seriously injured in the 2013 Boston Marathon Bombings. Jeff Bauman loses both his legs in the tragedy, but captures the hearts of his city and the world through his recovery, as he adjusts to his new way of life. Drama based on a true story, starring Jake Gyllenhaal

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a newlywed Olympian and his wife look for a home in Wiltshire on a budget of £525,000, and he also tries his hand at the little-known sport of sticke tennis

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dom Chinea offer DIY tips and tricks that won't break the bank

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: The first set of quarter-finalists take on a traditional dog groom when they gently removing the dead hairs from the coat of one of the UK's oldest breeds, the Welsh terrier, before producing a very modern look for the Dogwalk that requires smiley faces, flared legs and little outfits. Plus, in a new challenge, the contestants' knowledge of crossbreeds is put to the test as they try to identify a dog's parents. Presented by Sheridan Smith, with judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright leads a daily dose of workout sessions. Today he shows how to improve flexibility and balance

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: A duo from Somerset are among the contestants taking part in the antiques game show to curate the most valuable collection, with £1,000 going to the winners. Paul Martin hosts

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian search for bargains at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, where their interests are piqued by a vintage railway sign and an antique telescope

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison visits Britain's largest estuarine habitat in Morecambe Bay to discover the best-kept secrets of the winter landscape, including an unusual gentlemen's club of grey seals on Walney Island. She also reveals why both birds and fishermen are attracted to the area during the harsh weather

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day 11 of the Grand Slam tournament at Melbourne Park in Australia, featuring both women's semi-finals and the opening men's last-four match

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: During her final days of pregnancy, Rocket Dog searches for a new burrow in which to give birth, while Zorro experiences life as a Commando

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Rocket Dog's pups are put in jeopardy when babysitters Sophie and Axel become distracted by Wilson - before an even greater threat emerges

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Barrow Hill Roundhouse near Chesterfield in Derbyshire. Antiques experts Jonathan Pratt and Raj Bisram are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Jonathan comes across a collection of 1950s powder compacts, while Raj discovers some railway signal lamps. Paul finds out about the history of the Roundhouse and gets hands on with some locomotive maintenance

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Marcus Brigstocke, Anna Richardson, Tom Rosenthal and Sindhu Vee test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to cultural creatives who make other worlds for people's exploration and delight. Booker Prize-winner Marlon James discusses his Dark Star trilogy, which turns the fantasy genre on its head, and David Greig, artistic director of Edinburgh's Lyceum Theatre, takes viewers on a quest to an ancient woodland, where appealing to the spirits seems completely rational during a pandemic. Mary also chairs a debate on whether the occult is enjoying a revival or if it has always been more popular than she imagines

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares ideas for feasts throughout the day, with her savoury take on French toast an ideal choice for weekend brunch or a comforting and quick supper. With friends to entertain for dinner, she rustles up an informal Mediterranean dish of juicy spiced lamb kofta with garlic sauce, followed by rose and pepper pavlova with strawberries. There are also recipes for an aromatic golden egg curry and a white chocolate cheesecake

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Pam and Marcus have a big garden surrounded by large established trees, which stretches away from their suburban home in St Albans, Hertfordshire. Now their children have grown up, they want to transform the garden into a space they they can enjoy themselves. Designer Helen Elks-Smith is set on a design that begins with the home, while rival Tom Massey wants to transport them to another world. Which idea will the couple choose? Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit the Yorkshire Dales to work at Swinton Estate, the ancestral home of Lord and Lady Masham. By taking a look the heart of the grand house and its countryside charm, as well as the wider estate, which has opened up to for guests to enjoy, Monica and Giles learn about the challenges of making history pay in the modern world

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A musical review of 1990, the year that saw hip-hop and dance music hit the charts with a passion thanks to the likes of Adamski, Orbital, 808 State, Snap! and Eurodance sensations Snap! Betty Boo, Beats International and MC Tunes introduced the British take on hip-hop, Sinead O'Connor and Maria McKee took to the stage with big ballads and both John Barnes and Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles showed their rapping skills. Milli Vanilli got caught up in a singing scandal, and the battle for the Christmas number one saw a stand-off between Cliff Richard and Vanilla Ice

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. In the second programme, Trump turns his attention to the Middle East, where he takes America to the brink of war with Iran, shocks the world by pulling troops out of Syria, and announces breakthrough agreements between Israel and Arab states

programme.title: David Harewood: Psychosis and Me
programme.desc: At the age of 23, David Harewood had a psychotic breakdown and was sectioned. Two years ago he opened up on Twitter about the experience - and now he wants to tell the full story. Travelling back to his home town of Birmingham, the actor lets viewers into the realities of experiencing a psychotic breakdown, and how much he blocked out at the time, meeting up with his mother and old friends who were with him at the time. But this isn't just his story. He also spends time with other young people living with psychosis and the NHS professionals who treat them

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: The second part of this two-part documentary explores the initial outbreak of Covid-19 in the US. When Covid-19 first arrived on American soil, US federal health authorities were seemingly unaware of how transmissible the virus truly was. This account chronicles 54 days in which the United States made crucial decisions that would severely impact the spread of the virus. Featuring interviews with public health officials and scientists who had already concluded that the virus had global pandemic potential, who were confronted with a failing diagnostic test and a president who appeared to repeatedly undermine the true threat of the virus

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts a star-studded version of the culinary contest, which sees Mary Berry, Chris Bavin and Angela Hartnett judging 10 famous cooks. Taking part are Ferne McCann, Gareth Thomas, Desiree Burch, Ed Balls, Karim Zeroual, Ruth Madeley, Tom Read Wilson, Shobna Gulati, Ed Byrne and Rachel Johnson. In this first edition, Mary wants to know what home cooking means to the contestants, so she challenges them to produce their ultimate home-cooked dinner

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a spice box, two Chinese statues, a funfair horse, a 1900s cabinet and a dummy called Steve. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea's imaginations are put to the test when they make different versions of children's furnuiture. The duo also revamp an old lampshade and make a simple key holder. Plus, from the archives, Alan Titchmarsh returfs a lawn, and Joe Swift and Carol Klein reveal their favourite tools for gardening

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Newby Hall & Gardens in Ripon, North Yorkshire. Treasures include an intriguing letter from Lord Nelson, politely replying to his optician just weeks before the Battle of Trafalgar. There's also a piece of the set from Live Aid in 1985, rescued from a skip, and an engagement ring picked up in a pawn shop. The Bradford Museum also launches an appeal to find the families related to thousands of photos in their archive

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: A Feel Good Friday edition as Mark Wright brings fitness to secondary school children, leading a daily dose of workout sessions, joined by a host of his celebrity friends

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as two teams put their knowledge of antiques to the test, while competing to build the most valuable collection, for a chance to win £1,000

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian race towards the finish line at the showdown auction in London. Jonty finds a pair of valuable chairs in the back of a van, and Danny tries his luck on a showbiz tantalus once owned by Danny La Rue

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison concludes her exploration of Britain's winter landscapes with a trip to the Shetland archipelago. Here she witnesses the mesmerising beauty of the Northern Lights, encounters Viking practices alongside present-day crofting and learns about seal and otter pup rescues - each of which help prove life is far from dull for the islands' inhabitants during this bleak season

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from day 12 of the Grand Slam tournament at Melbourne Park in Australia, featuring the concluding men's semi-final

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Rocket Dog's pups are taken on an outing, but the trip becomes dangerous when a rival group shows up

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Maybelline and the Aztecs are forced to fight their way out of trouble when rivals the Lazuli appear at the Manor

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, where experts Caroline Hawley and James Lewis choose items to take to auction. James is drawn to a collection of First World War memorabilia and Caroline finds an exciting collection of postcards. Plus, Paul meets an artist whose work has led him back in time to the ancient art of relief printing

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Marcus Brigstocke, Anna Richardson, Tom Rosenthal and Sindhu Vee go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games for the final time, prior to the week's overall winner being declared

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions in a bid to win a jackpot of £3,000

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: The sex of a penguin can only be determined by a blood test, so the penguin keepers' annual sweepstake is launched to guess the sex of all the new youngsters. However, they've noticed that young Nessie has a spinal deformity and the vets are called in to investigate. New babies at both Edinburgh Zoo and the Highland Wildlife Park are keeping everyone busy - not just penguins, but lion cubs, caribou deer and parrots

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The host explores the scenery of the west coast of the United States, beginning in Arizona where he learns about cacti and marvels at the sight of them in the desert and in private gardens where they have replaced traditional plants and lawns. In Palm Springs, he views gardens with famous owners, from film stars to big businessmen and politicians. Los Angeles is Monty's next stop where he hears how schoolchildren are taking up gardening. His trip ends in Seattle where he visits a tropical getaway for one of the city's main workforces and views some beautiful green spaces just outside the city

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The art and cultural historian brings to life the huge personalities and larger-than-life stories behind each find - and reveals the surprising fact that man's understanding of each treasure is still shaped by the interpretations of these men and their eras, whether that's the height of the British Empire, or Europe in the grip of turn-of-the-20th century nationalistic fervour. In this episode, Janina heads to the blue seas and ancient palaces of the Greek island of Crete to discover the truth behind the myth of the minotaur

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools open the doors to his south London recording studio, chatting to musicians about their favourite pieces of music. Kicking off this latest series, he welcomes one of the year's most hotly tipped artists, singer-songwriter Arlo Parks, who performs a track from her critically acclaimed album Collapsed in Sunbeams. Plus, there is a performance by Kings of Leon from the band's studio, and Sleaford Mods and Billy Nomates showcase their single Mork n Mindy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Faisal Islam, the BBC's Economics Editor, reviews the day's important national and international news stories

programme.title: Muse: Simulation Theory (2020)
programme.desc: Concert film recorded London's O2 Arena featuring British rock band Muse and based on their 2018 album, which incorporates lighter influences from science fiction and 1980s pop culture. Conceived and recorded in September 2019, Simulation Theory follows a team of scientists as they investigate the source of a paranormal anomaly appearing around the world - releasing a deadly virus

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The team lets off steam in Adama's boxing tournament, but old grudges threaten relationships on board the ship when rivalry between Kara and Lee sets off a brutal match. Flashbacks from New Caprica reveal the events surrounding their enduring rift. Sci-fi drama, starring Edward James Olmos, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Thousands of people are left starving after the fleet's food supply is contaminated, forcing the team to find an alternative source or face extinction. To make matters worse, Athena stumbles across a planet rich in algae, but it can only be reached through a deadly radiation field. Sci-fi drama, starring Grace Park

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Marianna Spring looks at the scare tactics of anti-vaxxers, revealing the scale of a social media blitz that has targeted vulnerable people and is now reaching younger generations yet to be called for their vaccination. The programme witnesses the reaction of a test group exposed to one anti-vax video, all under the watchful eye of one of the UK's most respected doctors. Will they be influenced by disinformation or will their plans to be vaccinated remain unchanged?

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate the spookier side of nature, including a tree that bleeds, a fungus that likes to eat ant brains and a flesh-chewing fly

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Crashed spaceships, and parents being beamed aboard a UFO dressed as space aliens - brother and sister Hattie and Bertie are planning a wedding that's out of this world

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A science den for a budding astrophysicist

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve sets sail to Bali for a diving adventure to find a giant ocean sunfish

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: With the mob now numbering 50, the burrow is full to bursting point and Rocket Dog's leadership comes under threat. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Following the death of Whiskers leader Flower, Maybelline and Rocket Dog battle it out for dominance of their respective groups and strive to regain control over them. As winter approaches it looks as if the manor might not be big enough for both of them. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Wilson makes a move to join the Aztecs group, but when his advances are thwarted by Zaphod, he seeks the attention of a female from the Whiskers clan. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as the second group of quarter-finalists give some scruffy bichons frisés their trademark white cloud look, and try to identify a cross-breed's two parents. In the final challenge, they must trim their dogs' coats to create a homage to a place close to their hearts, before showing off their creations on the Dogwalk. But who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor take through to next week?

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the remote Stoer Peninsula in the North West Highlands, Helen and Graham are relieved to see tourists returning to the area, bringing a very welcome financial boost with them. However, they are soon forced to make a decision with devastating consequences. In Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan attempt to shear all their sheep, and in the Lake District, Matt and Murray embark on a dramatic gather

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the semi-final of the cooking challenge. Mary Berry challenges the remaining contestants to prepare a fish supper, Chris Bavin asks them to come up with a meal that puts mushrooms front and centre, and Angela Hartnett sets them the task of making crab ravioli with homemade pasta dough. After a dramatic end to the final challenge, one more celebrity leaves the kitchen

programme.title: The 39 Steps (1935)
programme.desc: A Canadian in London offers a safe hiding place to a British secret agent investigating a spy ring. However, when she is murdered, he realises he will be the prime suspect and goes on the run with the intention of exposing the real killers. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller based on John Buchan's novel, starring Robert Donat, Madeleine Carroll, Lucie Mannheim, Godfrey Tearle and Peggy Ashcroft

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship between director Alfred Hitchcock and his stars, featuring interviews with the man himself, Kim Novak, Tippi Hedren, Joan Fontaine, Janet Leigh and Sean Connery. Sylvia Syms narrates

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Amanda and Rustie meet a bride-to-be who invites the whole group to her Hindu wedding, so they head out to buy traditional clothing, with hilarious consequences. Miriam and Lionel discover Old Kochi has a surprising connection to their Jewish heritage, and a visit to the synagogue makes Miriam reflect on the impact her choice to marry outside of the Jewish faith had on her family. Later, Bill takes half the group along to one of the biggest festivals in Kerala, Puli Kali, which celebrates the tiger

programme.title: Tomorrowland: A World Beyond (2015)
programme.desc: A boy genius in the 1960s finds his way into a spectacular futuristic metropolis while attempting to promote his idea for a jetpack. Decades later, he has grown into a disillusioned adult, while a girl who has the talent and enthusiasm he has lost also finds her way into this strange new world, and together they attempt to uncover its secrets. Fantasy adventure, starring George Clooney, Britt Robertson and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The eight remaining interior designers take part in a commercial design challenge to transform a country wedding venue in Sussex. Presented by Alan Carr with Judge Michelle Ogundehin

programme.title: Perry and Croft: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The celebration of comedy writing duo David Croft and Jimmy Perry continues with an examination of how their sitcoms chronicled British people's attitudes to love and sex in the 20th century, looking at its depiction in Dad's Army, It Ain't Half Hot Mum, Hi-de-Hi! and You Rang, M'Lord?

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: A major from Area HQ decides Corporal Jones is too old to be in the Home Guard, asking him to prove his worth by completing an assault course - leaving the rest of the platoon hatching a plan to help him. Comedy first broadcast in 1968, starring Clive Dunn, Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Victoria Wood: A Bafta Tribute
programme.desc: Julie Walters, Lenny Henry, Richard E Grant and Jim Broadbent gather for a gala honouring the much-loved entertainer, with contributions from Peter Kay and French and Saunders. The celebration offers an opportunity to look back over her career with a combination of celebrity tributes and clips of her work, culminating with the presentation of the Bafta award

programme.title: Victoria Wood: At It Again
programme.desc: The much-loved comedian performs her award-winning stand-up show in front of an audience at London's Royal Albert Hall. Recorded during her At It Again tour in 2001, the gig features Victoria's ruminations on everything from Disneyland visitors to her hysterectomy

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Bren's mother wreaks havoc in the factory and incurs Stan's wrath by pursuing an ill-considered romance, while Anita organises a heated debate on the workers' current situation. Comedy, with Victoria Wood and Julie Walters

programme.title: Educating Rita (1983)
programme.desc: A Liverpool hairdresser unhappy with her marriage and her lot in life enrols on an Open University course hoping to expand her horizons. The lively pupil provides some much-needed inspiration for her boozy, world-weary tutor, but over time, he begins to question whether his influence is entirely positive. Comedy drama based on Willy Russell's stage play, starring Michael Caine and Julie Walters

programme.title: The Lighthouse (2016)
programme.desc: Two men are assigned to keep a lighthouse on an island in the Irish sea in 1801. A freak storm cuts the island off from the mainland for months on end, and the isolation drives both men to the brink of madness, with devastating consequences. Fact-based thriller, starring Mark Lewis Jones and Michael Jibson

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nicholas Crane explores the history of England's largest island, the Isle of Wight, learning how the fossil-rich area was once attached to the mainland and contains clues to the geographical journey made by the whole of Britain

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: The natural world at rest offers a constant source of calm and comfort, helping people to experience more being and less doing. Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe talks viewers through the process and onto an immersive journey around the sights and sounds of resting wildlife all over the planet

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape with a glowing evening sky on top of a black canvas

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James manages to settle into the routine of his new life - but then meets Helen, the beautiful daughter of a local farmer. Siegfried decides to bring some business-like discipline to the practice by employing the formidable Mrs Harbottle as secretary

programme.title: Edie (2017)
programme.desc: Seeking to fulfil a decades-old dream to climb a mountain in the Scottish Highlands, Edith Moore, an 83-year-old widow, escapes to Scotland, where she grudgingly accepts the help of Jonny, the manager of a struggling shop that sells outdoor gear. Drama, starring Sheila Hancock and Kevin Guthrie

programme.title: Sheila Hancock Brushes Up: The Art of Watercolours
programme.desc: The actress presents a history of watercolour painting, discovering a catalogue of little-known works and exploring how the medium's immediacy and freedom has given it an enduring appeal for both amateur and professional artists. She visits locations including the Alps, India and Venice to tell the stories behind paintings that were created there, as well as examining the importance of watercolours to artists in the days before photography

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank examines man-made wonders in Mexico and the USA, beginning with the Mayan burial pyramids of Palenque, where he uncovers stories of human sacrifice. Travelling north, he visits Mesa Verde, a settlement dug out of the cliffs by an unknown ancient civilisation, and the most famous American symbol - the Statue of Liberty. Also featured are the Toltec statues at Tula, Thomas Jefferson's house and the 1851 Navy Colt revolver

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about a husband-and-wife team who revolutionised broadcast news in America by capturing breaking news stories in Los Angeles from their helicopter. Their cameras captured not only the adrenaline of live news culture, but also the strain it began to take on their relationship, which ultimately led to a major life transition for one of them

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape with a glowing evening sky on top of a black canvas

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: The natural world at rest offers a constant source of calm and comfort, helping people to experience more being and less doing. Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe talks viewers through the process and onto an immersive journey around the sights and sounds of resting wildlife all over the planet

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on August 23, with performances by the Human League, Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers, Bombalurina, Mariah Carey, Tina Turner, Lindy Layton featuring Janet Kay, Cliff Richard, Deee-Lite and Deacon Blue

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on August 30, with performances by artists including Aswad, Sonia, the KLF, Betty Boo, Jason Donovan, Bombalurina, Whitesnake, Steve Miller Band and New Kids on the Block

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of hits by artists that were hardly heard from again after one of their songs climbed to the pinnacle of pop, alongside an analysis of the 'one-hit wonder' phenomenon. Featuring Peter Sarstedt's 1969 track Where Do You Go To (My Lovely)?, Trio's 1982 smash Da Da Da, Move Closer, by Phyllis Nelson, and You Get What You Give, by New Radicals

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable Top of the Pops performances from the 1980s, a time of outlandish hairstyles and dubious fashions. Featuring music by Adam Ant, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, Bucks Fizz, Yazz, Duran Duran, Wham! and Culture Club

programme.title: The Kemps: All True
programme.desc: Spoof documentary, starring Gary and Martin Kemp as themselves, following the brothers as they launch new careers in the wake of Spandau Ballet's 40th anniversary celebrations. While Martin tries to get a sci-fi movie franchise off the ground, Gary has a new brand of vegan meat substitute to promote

programme.title: Boy George's 1970s: Save Me from Suburbia
programme.desc: The BBC's My Generation season continues as Boy George revisits his teenage years. While history may remember the 1970s as being marked by strikes, poverty and civil discord, the singer reveals that - for the young George O'Dowd - the decade was the beginning of his musical, social and sexual coming-of-age. He discusses how sexual repression and disenfranchisement, along with the advent of glam rock, disco and punk, gave rise to the flamboyant persona he would later unleash on the world, via Culture Club's memorable first appearance on Top of the Pops in 1982

programme.title: The New Romantics - A Fine Romance
programme.desc: A look back at legends of the New Romantic era of the early 1980s, when outrageous fashions and make-up ruled the roost thanks to the influence of performers including Duran Duran, Spandau Ballet and Boy George. Narrated by Magenta Devine, with contributors including Boy George, Gary Kemp, Adam Ant, Nick Rhodes, Steve Strange, Rusty Egan, Marilyn, Jonathan Ross, Caryn Franklin, Fiona Bruce and Robert Elms

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Happy Mondays frontman Shaun Ryder and Creation Records boss Alan McGee reveal a selection of their all-time favourite tracks. From first jobs to private jets, the long-time friends unpack the songs that formed the soundtrack to their lives. Amid the eclectic tunes, Shaun also discovers his lost Tops of the Pops appearance and Alan declares an alternative Scottish national anthem. Theirs is a blistering playlist of indie, punk and ska classics from Buzzcocks to the Specials, Junior Murvin to Marc Bolan, Orange Juice to Underworld and many more

programme.title: Boy George's 1970s: Save Me from Suburbia
programme.desc: The BBC's My Generation season continues as Boy George revisits his teenage years. While history may remember the 1970s as being marked by strikes, poverty and civil discord, the singer reveals that - for the young George O'Dowd - the decade was the beginning of his musical, social and sexual coming-of-age. He discusses how sexual repression and disenfranchisement, along with the advent of glam rock, disco and punk, gave rise to the flamboyant persona he would later unleash on the world, via Culture Club's memorable first appearance on Top of the Pops in 1982

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of hits by artists that were hardly heard from again after one of their songs climbed to the pinnacle of pop, alongside an analysis of the 'one-hit wonder' phenomenon. Featuring Peter Sarstedt's 1969 track Where Do You Go To (My Lovely)?, Trio's 1982 smash Da Da Da, Move Closer, by Phyllis Nelson, and You Get What You Give, by New Radicals

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Following his adventures at Everest Base Camp, the intrepid traveller leaves the mountains behind and moves on to Tibet. Here he discovers that, while there may be an increasing Chinese influence, religion is once again being tolerated. He visits the Potala Palace, the spectacular monasteries of Tashilunpo and takes a dip in a hot spring. From there he continues his journey with a family of nomads heading to a summer horse festival in Yushu

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: A diary dating from 1943 is discovered hidden in an old bunker and Montalbano is entrusted with the dark secrets revealed in the diary and starts to research the time around which it was written. During his investigation, a 90-year-old businessman is found murdered in his own home and the detective starts to think that the events cannot possibly be unrelated. Italian crime drama, starring Luca Zingaretti

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane -to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

He solves a puzzle for Harry and Henry the Hedgehogs and encourages Brie the Bumblebee to get warm so she can fly. Brie is able to return the favour and points him in the right direction to meet Sid. After breakfast, Iffah and her brother Walli play together - he does a drawing while she plays with her toy duck. Iffah’s family go to an indoor play area, where Iffah has fun playing in the ball pool. When Myles and his dad arrive at great grandma’s house, Myles reads a book with grandma and even turns the pages all on his own! Both children love playing with their families - laughing, smiling, clapping, playing peek-a-boo and making noise. Bonnie gets excited when her dad suggests a day out to the beach! When they get there, Bonnie is concentrating on making sandcastles before splashing her feet in the water, laughing and loving the sea. Harry and his family go on a bug hunt at a nature reserve, but what will they find? While the family enjoys a relaxing picnic, Harry tries a banana for the very first time. Bonnie is happily crawling along the beach finding seashells before mum lays out a picnic with yummy hummus and pitta bread. He’s inadvertently taken a picture of himself instead of the weedy sea dragon. Just then, Mr Hammond calls to see how the downloading of the photos is going and specifically asks if they have seen his picture of the weedy sea dragon. programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. There is a turtle expert visiting the Safari World aquarium. Mr Hammond is on a scuba diving holiday in Borneo, so it’s up to Andy to ensure everything is ready for their arrival. Andy does a great job making the aquarium looking its best, but when Mr Hammond asks Andy if he has cleaned the green sea turtles, Andy is a little confused as he thought they cleaned themselves. Mr Hammond informs Jen and Andy that green sea turtles get clean by letting little fish nibble away the dirt and parasites on their skin and shell. However, he neglects to tell them what kind of fish, so Andy decides to find out for himself. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! programme.desc: Biography of Laurel and Hardy, focusing on the events of 1953 as the comedy duo try to re-ignite their film careers by embarking on a gruelling tour of British variety halls. As they set out on their demanding schedule, with their wives back home in the US, Stan is distracted by his attempts to pin down the producer of their proposed comeback film and Ollie tries to heal a rift caused by a perceived professional betrayal. Discovering the killers are rogue policemen willing to go to any lengths to cover up their crime, he is forced to go into hiding within the Amish community, experiencing a clash of cultures and unexpected feelings for the boy's widowed mother. programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Southampton v Chelsea. Everton were looking for their first win away to their neighbours since 1999 and will have drawn confidence from some recent home defeats for the Reds. Southampton were looking to get back on track after a humiliating 9-0 defeat at the hands of Manchester United 17 days ago, while the Blues were hoping to move up the table under new manager Thomas Tuchel. Plus, two crucial matches in the battle to avoid relegation - Burnley v West Bromwich Albion and Fulham v Sheffield United

programme.title: Stronger (2017)
programme.desc: A man's life changes radically after he is seriously injured in the 2013 Boston Marathon Bombings. Jeff Bauman loses both his legs in the tragedy, but captures the hearts of his city and the world through his recovery, as he adjusts to his new way of life. Drama based on a true story, starring Jake Gyllenhaal

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mother Sarah thinks it would be easier to raise her three-year-old triplets in Australia - but stay-at-home father Conor has major concerns. A trial week in Perth gives the pair the chance to experience what life could offer them - but is a move to Oz the right decision for this family's future? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda are determined to catch a wanted man who fled the island after killing his girlfriend, having learned he has returned to pay his respects to his dying father

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is property hunting in Shropshire with a couple of keen gardeners who want their £425,000 to buy them a country home with ample space for them and their hot tub. While in the county, Margherita samples some of Shropshire's pioneering local produce at Chadwell Park Farm, which has successfully turned its hand to making vegetable juices from its salad crops

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With the evidence stacked against him, Phil is running out of options - and things only get worse when Max starts piling on the pressure. programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as the second group of quarter-finalists give some scruffy bichons frisés their trademark white cloud look, and try to identify a cross-breed's two parents. In the final challenge, they must trim their dogs' coats to create a homage to a place close to their hearts, before showing off their creations on the Dogwalk. But who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor take through to next week?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A man confesses to murdering his boss, despite being unable to remember what happened. Convinced that things are not as simple as they seem, Neville determines to uncover the truth and unmask the real killer. Detective drama, starring Ralf Little

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate. On the panel are MPs Mark Harper, Nadia Whittome and Layla Moran, alongside Linda Bauld, Professor of Public Health at the University of Edinburgh, and Peter Borg-Neal, the CEO of Oakman Inns

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles talks about jewellery with expert Izzie Balmer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Beth Hood wants to start a new life for her family down under, but her husband Ben is not convinced, so they decide to spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia

programme.title: Critical Incident
