programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Ines sends detectives Max and Miranda to the home of famous German reality TV host and record producer Jurgen Kuhl to investigate a report that equipment is being stolen. Max finds he has unwittingly proposed to his long-term girlfriend Carmen, while Miranda is also offered a proposal - one she'd be mad to refuse

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by fulfilling a lifetime's ambition to visit Japan, the home of their favourite food. The duo sample sushi at Tokyo's famous fish market and have the privilege of spending time in a sumo stable, where they take part in a wrestling bout

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley goes on a house-hunting mission to help a couple with a £525,000 budget find a country home in north Cornwall to retire to. Along the way, she visits the annual Royal Cornwall Show, which has showcased the county's heritage for over 220 years. While there, Ginny samples the traditional nettle-covered yarg cheese and sees if a very rare breed of sheep manages to win a best-in-class rosette

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea shows a crafty make to hang bathroom towels on, while Jay Blades builds an under-bed storage solution. Plus, Monty Don gives us the lowdown on the best hedge trimming tools, a dated armchair is given an outrageous upcycle, and Alan Titchmarsh shares his secrets on how to build a pond

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on a fairground dodgem, two figurines, a pair of ornate candlesticks, a vintage garden sprayer and a rescued mannequin

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd round off the week with a lively mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Eve and Poppy combine forces to defeat Cameron. Alex tries to talk to Amber about her drastic change in behaviour, and Stevie hires Sonny to help spruce up the Two Jailbirds

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack steps in to try to diffuse the situation between Max and Mick, but when he realises what his brother has done, his blood runs cold. Meanwhile, Max tells Linda that they cannot move on if Max is around. Elsewhere, Stacey has a plan to prove that Ruby is lying and tells Kush to meet her at the club

programme.title: Stan & Ollie (2018)
programme.desc: Biography of Laurel and Hardy, focusing on the events of 1953 as the comedy duo try to re-ignite their film careers by embarking on a gruelling tour of British variety halls. As they set out on their demanding schedule, with their wives back home in the US, Stan is distracted by his attempts to pin down the producer of their proposed comeback film and Ollie tries to heal a rift caused by a perceived professional betrayal. The build-up of pressure starts to tarnish their precious partnership. Steve Coogan and John C Reilly star

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by guests Oscar-nominated British star Rosamund Pike, who discusses her new comedy thriller I Care a Lot. Acerbic chef Gordon Ramsay chats about his new BBC quiz show Bank Balance, while Welsh rugby union great Gareth Thomas reflects on his honest and challenging new book, Stronger. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville talks about playing Roald Dahl in To Olivia, while comedian and presenter Judi Love also guests. Plus, Ella Henderson and Tom Grennan perform Let's Go Home Together

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby, former Everton midfielder Leon Osman, ex-Great Britain swimmer Fran Halsall and Olympic 4x400m bronze medallist Anyika Onuora join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows. The panel is joined by social media's Robert Borthwick, a man known for his wit and whimsy on Twitter that riles and rallies fans of every club in equal measure

programme.title: Witness (1985)
programme.desc: A Philadelphia cop is assigned to protect an Amish boy who was the only witness to a murder. Discovering the killers are rogue policemen willing to go to any lengths to cover up their crime, he is forced to go into hiding within the Amish community, experiencing a clash of cultures and unexpected feelings for the boy's widowed mother. Thriller, directed by Peter Weir, with Harrison Ford, Kelly McGillis, Lukas Haas and Danny Glover

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Yotam Ottolenghi, and special guests Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt. Plus, Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton and Arsenal v Manchester City

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the meeting in Torun, Poland, including the women's 60m, 3,000m and 800m, and men's 60m hurdles, pole vault and 1500m. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from the women's singles final at Melbourne Park in Australia, which was won by Sofia Kenin last year

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Retired couple David and Joyce's back garden in their home outside Gloucester looks more like an allotment than a garden. David is a dedicated vegetable grower and for Joyce, that means there's nowhere shady or pretty to sit and enjoy the garden. Meanwhile, David's increasing back and knee problems mean he is struggling to keep up its maintenance. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set out to make the garden both prettier and more user friendly, all within a £3,000 budget

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie is joined by Steven Thompson to present the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Impressionist Jon Culshaw, Richie Anderson from BBC Radio 2, broadcaster Rachel Johnson and actor Denis Lawson answer questions on Doctor Who, The Saturdays, The Laconia incident and Miles Davis

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, England goalkeepers David James and Siobhan Chamberlain take on rugby legend James Haskell and fitness coach Chloe Madeley

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees footballing legend John Barnes and football pundit Chris Kamara teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Peter Purves, Lesley Judd, Jane Tucker, Freddy Marks, Dave Benson Phillips, Mike Read, Kirsten O'Brien and Terrence Hardiman

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha and Ethan have kept their distance since Fenisha had the baby, but when their son is rushed into hospital, Fenisha realises that Ethan will be there to support her. Meanwhile, Connie is shocked by Grace's behaviour, and Rosa encourages Ollie to make a move. Starring George Rainsford, Olivia D'Lima and Amanda Mealing

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton is attacked while on remand in prison awaiting trial and tells Fleming she had a relationship with a high-ranking officer - who may have reason to want her framed. After AC-12 gains access to the files on the protected witness, the evidence suggests a possible police conspiracy and the cohesion of the team is further threatened by the arrival of a former adversary. Crime drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Southampton v Chelsea. The contestants are Peter Purves, Lesley Judd, Jane Tucker, Freddy Marks, Dave Benson Phillips, Mike Read, Kirsten O'Brien and Terrence Hardiman

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha and Ethan have kept their distance since Fenisha had the baby, but when their son is rushed into hospital, Fenisha realises that Ethan will be there to support her. Meanwhile, Connie is shocked by Grace's behaviour, and Rosa encourages Ollie to make a move. Starring George Rainsford, Olivia D'Lima and Amanda Mealing

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton is attacked while on remand in prison awaiting trial and tells Fleming she had a relationship with a high-ranking officer - who may have reason to want her framed. After AC-12 gains access to the files on the protected witness, the evidence suggests a possible police conspiracy and the cohesion of the team is further threatened by the arrival of a former adversary. Crime drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Southampton v Chelsea. Everton were looking for their first win away to their neighbours since 1999 and will have drawn confidence from some recent home defeats for the Reds. Southampton were looking to get back on track after a humiliating 9-0 defeat at the hands of Manchester United 17 days ago, while the Blues were hoping to move up the table under new manager Thomas Tuchel. Plus, two crucial matches in the battle to avoid relegation - Burnley v West Bromwich Albion and Fulham v Sheffield United

programme.title: Stronger (2017)
programme.desc: A man's life changes radically after he is seriously injured in the 2013 Boston Marathon Bombings. Jeff Bauman loses both his legs in the tragedy, but captures the hearts of his city and the world through his recovery, as he adjusts to his new way of life. Drama based on a true story, starring Jake Gyllenhaal

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Human rights activist Azimjan Askarov was imprisoned in Kyrgyzstan in 2010 for a crime he says he did not commit. Ever since then, his wife Hadicha has campaigned tirelessly for his release. Now she sees one last chance for justice, in an appeal hearing at the country's supreme court. But as Hadicha prepares for the verdict, coronavirus is spreading across Kyrgyzstan, stopping Hadicha's prison visits and putting 69-year-old Azimjan at risk. Our World follows an extraordinary story of love, courage - and never giving up.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Lucy Hedges looks at Dubai's efforts to reschedule its mammoth 2020 World Expo, which was postponed during the pandemic. The team also catches up with Cuba's enigmatic shoeshine poet, checks out the typewriter tycoon whose fortunes changed one part of northern Italy and meets the South Korean robot that can peer into the future.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Marianna Spring looks at the scare tactics of anti-vaxxers, revealing the scale of a social media blitz that has targeted vulnerable people and is now reaching younger generations yet to be called for their vaccination. The programme witnesses the reaction of a test group exposed to one anti-vax video, all under the watchful eye of one of the UK's most respected doctors. Will they be influenced by disinformation or will their plans to be vaccinated remain unchanged?

programme.desc: Calum Maclean visits Spar Cave on the Isle of Skye, finding that even reaching his destination proves tricky

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: St Mirren v Livingston. Delayed full-match coverage of the clash at St Mirren Park. With commentary by Alex O'Henley

programme.title: ALT
programme.desc: Including a live performance from Aidan Moffat & RM Hubbert, and chat from Ricky Ross and Dougie Vipond from Deacon Blue. With Dòmhnall Eoghainn Mackinnon

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Ste is in court to claim £205.50 for two water filter bottles that were loaned to a friend, but were not returned. Maryleen is in court to claim £420 for the outstanding amount owed on various loans she made to her daughter, while Conor wishes to claim £1,000 outstanding on a TV he bought with his brother and friend, who he claims have refused to pay their full share. Criminal barrister Robert Rinder considers the validity of each person's argument

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five amateur dramatic enthusiasts compete in the game show, taking it in turns to find the answers to top 10 lists, before trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on a large arcade-style 'coin-pusher' machine. The more counters they collect, the greater the prize fund, with the last one standing competing for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Rob, Cydney, Pauline and Joel pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gabby takes a test, Meena is mortified, and Jamie confesses his feelings

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: In just nine years new petrol and diesel cars will be banned in the UK. It's a big 'green' move by the government. But what does it mean for drivers - and the car industry itself? Motoring journalist Ginny Buckley investigates

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate is squeamish, Tracy is sceptical, and David expresses his concerns to Pollard

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Former boxer Chris Eubank talks about his private life and career, touching on his teenage years as a shoplifter, his divorce, bankruptcy and his victory in 1990 over fierce rival Nigel Benn. Chris also recalls the 1991 fight in which his opponent Michael Watson received a near-fatal brain injury, and discusses the success of his son, Chris Eubank Jr

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: 56 Up
programme.desc: Another chance to see the 2012 instalment of the landmark documentary series, featuring an update on a group of participants whose lives have been chronicled since 1964, when they were seven years old. Michael Apted catches up with Sue Davis, Paul Kligerman, Neil Hughes and Peter Davies, finding out whether they have achieved their ambitions

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives, following a woman on a quest to solve a family mystery and find her sisters, and a woman living with the heart-breaking decision to give up her son for adoption when she was just a teenager

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: Alice decides to reject Tanvi's offer - Harry would have wanted Alice to develop the land herself with the support of their family. But it's not long before the land project is overshadowed, as Nicola blurts out that Alice is also hoping to try for Harry's baby using the sperm that he mysteriously stored before he died. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Judge Rinder

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A team of five players answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate feels guilty, Laurel is determined to be supportive, and Vinny and Liv are all loved up

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne shows Simon another feather from Oliver and asserts that it's his way of letting her know that he's fine. However, Nick is concerned by her behaviour. Abi and Kevin celebrate their engagement, and the arrival of Ed's brother Ronnie puts Aggie on edge

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Host Jeremy Clarkson may also lend a hand, if the players trust his general knowledge

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the final of the singing contest in which the panel and the studio audience must guess the last three famous faces behind the elaborate costumes. Regular panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan are joined by last year's winner Nicola Roberts, while all 12 of this year's masked singers will be returning for a grand finale

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: In light of their momentous kiss, Cole wants to talk to his ex Natalie about the future direction of their relationship. However, a spanner is thrown in his plans after he bumps into new Assistant District Attorney Erica Malick, who he immediately ruffles feathers with. Meanwhile, after being kicked out of the house for Trish's girl's night, Murtaugh finds friendship in an unexpected place with Elliot, the witness that Cole sticks him with. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Nishi Munshi

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave demands to go on a foreign exchange. He soon finds himself headed for a week-long stay in a flat a few doors down.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: George finds a computer called Val but Val has a mind of her own. Soon she's set about uploading embarrassing videos to social media.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Gordon responds to an emergency along the Supreme Barrier Reef. The Tracy brothers once again set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood in an updated version of the show

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Fast flowing waters await Danny in today's dare as he takes on a white-water rafting course including the UK's steepest man-made drop.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean arranges a last-minute birthday treat for Teddy, including a morning in a toy shop and burgers for lunch. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean's landlady orders him to get rid of a mole making a mess of her exquisitely manicured croquet lawn

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: There's a new sheriff in town when Mike attempts to stop the raccoons from using Tom's train set to ship their loot back to their den.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series based on the popular film series. Mavis is 115 years old which is the age a Dracula finally earns their cape. But it won't be easy.

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po and the Kids make it to Gongmen City in search of the contact that they heard has some information about the airship crash - meanwhile, Xiao, mourning the presumed death of her new panda friends, takes off into the woods and meets a new one.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by singer-songwriter Amy Macdonald and chefs Brian Turner and Paul Ainsworth. James prepares carrot soup and banoffee pithivier, and provides a masterclass in filleting fish

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his favourite classic French dishes, from steak and red wine jus, to a heritage salad with tomatoes and mozzarella straight from his gardens. He also receives an unexpected visit from Jean-Christophe Novelli, who prepares him a delicious Mouclade Des Boucholeurs

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The east coast stretch of his journey takes James to The Hamptons. Known as being the playground of wealthy New Yorkers, the area has a rich farming history. James cooks for some local farmers before going out on a lobster boat and cooking along the waterside

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani presents coverage of four races from Ascot, including the 3.35 Grade One Betfair Chase. Plus, two races from Haydock Park, including the 2.40 Grand National Trial, and the Kingwell Hurdle from Wincanton. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: A chance to relive some of the most dramatic end games in the arcade-themed quiz show's history, including agonising trades and jackpot counters teetering on the edge. Presented by Ben Shephard

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Chris, Claire, Steve and Jane pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with contestants Tom Allen, Fleur East and Simon Gregson all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo's entertainment show returns for a new series. Gary Barlow is live in the studio for an incredible End of The Show Show, Stephen Mulhern returns for an epic Ant v Dec, Fleur East is ready to give one viewer the surprise of their lives, and Harry and Jamie Redknapp are the victims of a brand new I'm A Celebrity Get Out Of Me Ear

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the first of this year's Battle Rounds in the singing contest. Coaches will.i.am, Tom Jones, Olly Murs and Anne-Marie all return to their infamous red chairs as the acts they have chosen from the blind auditions go head to head and fight for their place on their coach's team. The singers that impress their coach the most remain in the competition with a chance of winning a recording contract. Will those that aren't so lucky be stolen by another coach or will they be going home?

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Amongst the guests featured are Oprah Winfrey, Boris Johnson, Sandra Bullock, Tom Hanks, One Direction, Michael McIntyre, Dame Joan Collins, Big Narstie, Danny Dyer, James Corden, Carrie Fisher, Russell Howard and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Tomorrow Never Dies (1997)
programme.desc: James Bond joins forces with a Chinese agent to foil a media mogul's scheme to start a war between their countries - a case complicated by the fact that an old flame is now married to his new enemy. Using an undetectable stealth ship, the press baron plans to launch an undetectable sneak attack on both nations' navies, knowing they will blame each other - giving him exclusive global media coverage of the resulting conflict. Spy adventure, starring Pierce Brosnan, Jonathan Pryce, Teri Hatcher and Michelle Yeoh

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo's entertainment show returns for a new series. Gary Barlow is live in the studio for an incredible End of The Show Show, Stephen Mulhern returns for an epic Ant v Dec, Fleur East is ready to give one viewer the surprise of their lives, and Harry and Jamie Redknapp are the victims of a brand new I'm A Celebrity Get Out Of Me Ear

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the first of this year's Battle Rounds in the singing contest. Coaches will.i.am, Tom Jones, Olly Murs and Anne-Marie all return to their infamous red chairs as the acts they have chosen from the blind auditions go head to head and fight for their place on their coach's team. The singers that impress their coach the most remain in the competition with a chance of winning a recording contract. Will those that aren't so lucky be stolen by another coach or will they be going home?

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Amongst the guests featured are Oprah Winfrey, Boris Johnson, Sandra Bullock, Tom Hanks, One Direction, Michael McIntyre, Dame Joan Collins, Big Narstie, Danny Dyer, James Corden, Carrie Fisher, Russell Howard and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Tomorrow Never Dies (1997)
programme.desc: James Bond joins forces with a Chinese agent to foil a media mogul's scheme to start a war between their countries - a case complicated by the fact that an old flame is now married to his new enemy. Using an undetectable stealth ship, the press baron plans to launch an undetectable sneak attack on both nations' navies, knowing they will blame each other - giving him exclusive global media coverage of the resulting conflict. Spy adventure, starring Pierce Brosnan, Jonathan Pryce, Teri Hatcher and Michelle Yeoh

programme.title: The Perfect Man (2005)
programme.desc: Teenager Holly Hamilton grows tired of moving home every time her mother splits up with another boyfriend. When the family travels to New York, Holly is determined to stay in one place and vows to find her mum the ideal partner - even if she has to make him up. However, when the relationship starts to take off, Holly is faced with the impossible task of producing a perfect man to live up to her mother's inflated expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Hilary Duff, Heather Locklear and Chris Noth

She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth

Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.desc: Oscar's golfing pal asks him to investigate the disappearance of his son, who left home after a blazing row with his overbearing father and has been spotted frequenting a club in Whitby. Drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.desc: Martin tells Nick he is worried about him getting involved with Leanne. Mike is furious to discover Angie has blown the contract. Sally breaks down and is comforted by Chris

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate when the body of a woman is pulled from the river during the local Oak Apple carnival. Closer inspection suggests the drowning was deliberate - and, as the detectives find out more about the villagers, they learn the killing could be linked to the death of the victim's daughter eight years previously. John Nettles and Jason Hughes play the intrepid policemen, with guest star Simon Callow

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth receives several anonymous letters outlining a killer's intention to murder a succession of people in alphabetical order, prompting him to try to unmask the culprit before the body-count mounts. Crime drama based on the Agatha Christie novel, starring David Suchet, with Pippa Guard, Donald Sumpter and Miranda Forbes

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Pauline grows increasingly frustrated with her role at the surgery and starts to interfere with Martin's work, much to the GP's annoyance. The former surgeon talks to Bert, who is feeling down after his son Al's decision to go travelling, before coming face to face with the village's new police officer. Comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Bert comes to the conclusion that a new challenge will help him move on from Al's departure and decides to open a restaurant, but a spate of food-poisoning cases coincides with his first day of business. Martin meets Poppy, the surgery's new receptionist, and Pauline returns from her course. Comedy drama, starring Ian McNeice and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in Bath, where the wildly ambitious DCI McDonald finds herself paired with the shy, modest DS Dodds, who has languished on the shelf for most of his working life. However, it soon turns out the unassuming sergeant has a hidden talent for puzzle solving. Their first case sees the pair looking into the shooting of a homeless man in the deserted mansion of local industrialist Max Crockett. The Crockett appear to be a close-knit group but it soon becomes clear they have secrets to hide. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star as the detectives, with a guest appearance from Robert Lindsay

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Michael Kitchen returns to the role of Christopher Foyle, senior intelligence officer for MI5, in the first of three films. Foyle is drawn into the world of corrupt Nazi businessmen when a university professor is found murdered in a London park. When it transpires the victim was working as a translator in Nuremberg, the detective realises there are powerful people trying to conceal secrets from the Second World War. Meanwhile, Sam (Honeysuckle Weeks) volunteers for a risky undercover job, despite being pregnant. Guest stars include John Mahoney (Frasier), Jaime Winstone (Mad Dogs) and Nigel Lindsay (Four Lions)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid resolves to exercise a little discipline when Sally adopts a stray dog. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita struggles alone at work when Judy calls in sick. Percy shocks Emily by announcing that he is moving out. Gail is horrified when Nick takes Leanne out

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy and Butch announce their plan to get married. Steve heads to the hospital to silence Kathy. A fire breaks out at the Woolpack

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: A housing shortage forces a compromise between the flatmates and their disapproving landlord. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. Stan Bickle arrives in Aidensfield, bringing his daughter Josie (guest star Jenna Boyd), whose flirtatious ways steal David's heart and bring out the worst in Peggy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The full extent of the damage to the Woolpack is revealed. Kim gives Steve 10,000 pounds to run away with. Kathy tells the police that she saw Steve drive into her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is questioned about the theft. Kelly is shocked at Mandy's news about her marriage to Butch. Ned gives Robert boxing lessons to help him stand up to bullies

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick signs up to study PE at the college. Emily is upset at Percy's departure. Jon lies to Deirdre and goes back home to his wife Linda for the night

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail hopes Nick will stop seeing Leanne. Angie sobers up, and is horrified that she has moved in with Curly. Sally gets herself dressed up, hoping to spend the evening with Chris

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid wants a lie-in, but it is doomed from the beginning as Sally chases romance and Jean goes on a diet. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The age gap between Sid and his children becomes apparent when he returns home early from work. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin and Jo become concerned when Chrissy starts dating an older man - so they decide to play gooseberry. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo invite a German guest to their dinner party - but Mr Roper demonstrates that the Second World War has not ended as far as he is concerned

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar's golfing pal asks him to investigate the disappearance of his son, who left home after a blazing row with his overbearing father and has been spotted frequenting a club in Whitby. Meanwhile, the Aidensfield police officers are baffled by the activities of a modern-day Robin Hood, who has committed a string of burglaries and given away his ill-gotten gains to the elderly residents of a new block of flats. Drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim hands over information from Steve's office to the police. Jack is furious when Robert punches Donna in the face. Steve tells Kim that he is going to plead guilty

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The temporary Woolpack in the village hall starts taking shape. Mrs Kirk surprises Paddy with a free trip to Venice. Spalding threatens Steve with an attempted murder charge

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin tells Nick he is worried about him getting involved with Leanne. Mike is furious to discover Angie has blown the contract. Sally breaks down and is comforted by Chris

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally and Chris spend the night together. Angie is upset by Mike's attitude. Nick informs Gail that he is finished with Leanne. Jon tells Deirdre about his promotion at work

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate when the body of a woman is pulled from the river during the local Oak Apple carnival. Closer inspection suggests the drowning was deliberate - and, as the detectives find out more about the villagers, they learn the killing could be linked to the death of the victim's daughter eight years previously. John Nettles and Jason Hughes play the intrepid policemen, with guest star Simon Callow

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth receives several anonymous letters outlining a killer's intention to murder a succession of people in alphabetical order, prompting him to try to unmask the culprit before the body-count mounts. Crime drama based on the Agatha Christie novel, starring David Suchet, with Pippa Guard, Donald Sumpter and Miranda Forbes

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Pauline grows increasingly frustrated with her role at the surgery and starts to interfere with Martin's work, much to the GP's annoyance. The former surgeon talks to Bert, who is feeling down after his son Al's decision to go travelling, before coming face to face with the village's new police officer. Comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Bert comes to the conclusion that a new challenge will help him move on from Al's departure and decides to open a restaurant, but a spate of food-poisoning cases coincides with his first day of business. Martin meets Poppy, the surgery's new receptionist, and Pauline returns from her course. Comedy drama, starring Ian McNeice and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons

programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.desc: The duo find themselves the target of a powerful diamond merchant, forcing Dempsey to enlist the aid of a former boxer he put behind bars

Karl Howman guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.desc: Steed acquires an antique dagger that is said to be a clue to the whereabouts of a priceless treasure. However, everyone who has previously owned the knife has died. Stratford John and Ronald Lacey guest star

programme.desc: When Ray's van breaks down, Arthur takes to the water using a barge to transport a cargo of dodgy video recorders. However, the fearsome Bible-quoting Welshman who owns them is on his tail. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole rides an African Twin through the backroads of Oxfordshire, road-tests his first ever electric motorbike, and reveals Alan Milyard's extraordinary new Kawasaki

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Captain Jack Crocker, a robust seafaring man, is implicated in the murder of beautiful aristocrat Lady Brackenstall's abusive husband. Drama, guest starring Oliver Tobias, with Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke and Anne Louise Lambert

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: An attempt on her colleague's life scares Makepeace and she threatens to resign, but she may be hiding her true feelings. Detective drama, starring Glynis Barber and Michael Brandon

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan gets caught in the crossfire between the family of a recently deceased gangland leader and the members of the late man's crime syndicate, regarding the division of the hoodlum's wealth. The hitherto innocent son manages to play-off his parent's ex-cohorts against one another with increasing dexterity and appears only too keen to pick up the duplicitous traits of his late father. Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed recuperates in a private hospital after breaking his leg, unaware an old enemy whom he sent to prison is plotting to murder him. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star, with Ray Brooks

programme.desc: Arthur's plans to rig the Winchester's quiz night go awry when the questionmaster, an escaped convict, surrenders himself to the police officers present after suffering a turn. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.desc: Henry brings his Chopper rebuild to a glorious climax while taking time to ride the latest CCM bike, tell the story of the Honda Fireblade and ride out on vintage Norton ES2

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five postal workers from Morecambe show their knowledge of top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. A series of VIPs travel through Heathrow Airport terminals. Meanwhile, a team of hockey champions try to use their gold medals to get an upgrade

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. programme.desc: A second-rate ice-hockey player with a thuggish temperament discovers he has a powerful golf swing. Comedy, with Adam Sandler, Christopher McDonald, Carl Weathers, Julie Bowen and Frances Bay

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is asked by the former king of Fedyra to accompany him on an important mission in Switzerland, where he becomes aide to a queen

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. He disapproves of his daughter's villainous boyfriend, and Regan suspects he is being exploited to help break the romantic union. However, an even darker plan is being hatched, with the daughter an innocent pawn in a robbery. Kenny Lynch and Derek Francis guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: A home for retired clowns is not all smiles and cream pies as Steed and Tara King look into the killing of the Directors of the Capitol Land and Development Company. John Cleese and Bernard Cribbins guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur buys a hot-air balloon and attempts to break into aerial advertising. However, he is later abducted by a psychotic business rival who takes him to a disused power station, leaving Ray with the puzzle of how to track him down. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A crooked foreign diplomat helps arrange the theft of a consignment of silver bullion being transported through Tilbury docks. Bodie and Doyle are assigned to investigate, and it becomes apparent that they have a useful link to the villains via Doyle's girlfriend Deborah. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a family of five answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as impressionist Rory Bremner, weather reporter Laura Tobin, Dragons' Den's Deborah Meaden and TV gardener and chat show host Alan Titchmarsh pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed acquires an antique dagger that is said to be a clue to the whereabouts of a priceless treasure. However, everyone who has previously owned the knife has died. Stratford John and Ronald Lacey guest star

programme.desc: When Ray's van breaks down, Arthur takes to the water using a barge to transport a cargo of dodgy video recorders. However, the fearsome Bible-quoting Welshman who owns them is on his tail. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.desc: Captain Jack Crocker, a robust seafaring man, is implicated in the murder of beautiful aristocrat Lady Brackenstall's abusive husband. Drama, guest starring Oliver Tobias, with Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke and Anne Louise Lambert

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace

programme.title: The Sweeney

Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed recuperates in a private hospital after breaking his leg, unaware an old enemy whom he sent to prison is plotting to murder him. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star, with Ray Brooks

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Officer Estefania pushes passengers through security with a unique handling method, while drugs dog Cole works to protect the border

Joining her are tough-talking prison transport officer Tina, dreadlocked farm-dweller Stephen, superior IT manager David and glamorous beauty-school teacher Ashleigh. Alliances are quickly forged when David and Tina discover they both love a good gossip, before they turn their attention to Stephen when David compares him to a tramp. Julie overcooks her beef, but will her hosting make up for it and bag her the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Mend It for Money
programme.desc: Recruitment director Mario brings in a Mobo Bronco play horse, affectionately named Dobbin. This must-have toy was all the rage in the 1950s and '60s, and has been in the family for 70 years. However, Mario now would like it to find a new home and raise some money for charity in the process. Plus, agricultural student Amy looks for a furniture restorer to bring a pair of 19th-century corner chairs back to life

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Comedian Rob Beckett joins Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty in their cafe on Southend Pier. Rob's perfect meal out is 'duck on tap' at an all-you-can-eat Chinese buffet. He makes Peking-style roast duck, served with cucumber batons, hoisin sauce and the thinnest ever pancakes. Rob is also turned into a human NutriBullet as he attempts to break a world record. Jamie serves up a comforting chicken and mushroom pie, while Jimmy spices up Friday night snacks with his potato spirals and stuffed jalapeños wrapped in bacon. Away from the pier, the hosts are put to work at a watercress farm in Hampshire

programme.title: It's a Sin
programme.desc: November 1991 finds the situation darkening. Jill and Roscoe work extra hours, while Ritchie clings to past success and even Carol finds a kinder side. As the final days of the old gang unravel, everyone must face up to the spectre of Aids and the prejudice and fear that walk alongside it. When Valerie goes on the warpath, terrible confrontations are made and it falls to Jill to find hope and reconciliation for Ritchie in the darkest days of their lives. The final instalment of Russell T Davies' drama, starring Olly Alexander, Keeley Hawes and Lydia West

programme.title: The Last Leg

programme.title: Ramy
programme.desc: Now working for his uncle, Ramy meets a young Jewish woman through work and hooks up with her at a party. However, when they get close, his morally questionable cherry-picking approach to sex and drugs is called up. Comedy drama, starring Ramy Youssef

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Robert Popper's low-key comedy returns with Jonny in a foul mood, as he discovers a secret betrayal while reading Adam's childhood diary - and vows to get revenge. programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Grandma introduces the family to her new boyfriend, but the old curmudgeon quickly upsets everyone with his rude behaviour. Dad cannot stand him and hides in his shed, but nobody is prepared for what happens when the guest suddenly loses his temper. Comedy, starring Simon Bird

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Jonny claims to have dislocated his shoulder to get out of helping clean out the loft. Meanwhile, the boys' parents are not on speaking terms after an argument, with Dad revealing only that it is about something 'sexual'

programme.title: We Are the Best! (2014)

They find an unexpected kindred spirit in a born-again Christian girl, who has the musical talent they need. Drama based on Coco Moodysson's graphic novel, starring Mira Barkhammar, Mira Grosin and Liv LeMoyne. In Swedish

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring actor Daniel hosts the second dinner party in Perth, and hopes to impress his fellow contestants with a twist on the traditional Scottish menu - serving spaghetti hagginaise for his main and soft-drink flavoured ice-cream for dessert. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: The friends fight over a ring they believe was used in the Lord of the Rings film trilogy. Leonard hopes to return the item to its rightful owner - but Raj wishes to sell it so he can buy a jet ski, and Sheldon develops a Gollum-like obsession with the precious object

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover £45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a £4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim

programme.title: Esther Rantzen: Living with Grief
programme.desc: Campaigner and presenter Esther Rantzen sets out to explore the experience of bereavement and coping with grief. It's exactly 20 years since the death of Esther's husband, the broadcaster Desmond Wilcox, but in many ways she has yet to come to terms with her loss. Here she enlists the help of six individuals who each have their own story to tell, from a woman who lost her husband to Covid-19 and a mother whose child drowned, to a young man who was just 11 when his brother was murdered

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: David and Karl spring into action to treat Bea as her organs begin to shut down. Nicolette finally gets a win as her new lawyer takes charge of her legal issues

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ziggy tells Dean there is no chance they will get back together, while Jasmine bumps into an old friend from university at the prison. Martha tells Alf about Kieran's father

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Jon Bentley takes to the skies, testing out consumer drones that not only promise to be friendly to the domestic pocket, but are easy to fly and can record top-notch video. Armed with a budget and a premium Sonos soundbar, Jon and Georgie Barrat battle it out to see if there's as much difference in performance as the price tag suggests. Plus, Ortis Deley brings a rundown of his top five bike accessories and Harry Wallop demonstrates how claiming compensation can be as simple as a click of a mouse. Presented by Craig Charles

programme.title: Kate Humble's Coastal Britain
programme.desc: The naturalist enjoys an 11-mile walk in Exmoor along the South West Coast Path, regarded as one of the most beautiful such paths in the world. Starting at Porlock Weir, Kate heads to St Bueno's, a tiny parish church that seats just 33 worshippers - and which holds a dark past. She then heads inland to see the valley that inspired RD Blackmore's 1869 novel of outlaws and revenge, Lorna Doone. At Lynmouth, Kate rides the world's highest and steepest water-powered railway, before finishing her walk seemingly on top of the world in the Valley of Rocks

programme.title: Gregg Wallace: Big Weekends Away
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter visits Italian capital Rome, which boasts inexpensive cuisine and an unrivalled history that stretches back thousands of years. After checking out some its best sights on the back of a scooter and going inside the famous Trevi Fountain, Gregg heads to a restaurant popular with the locals to discover how, in the Eternal City, the best food is often the cheapest. He also learns the secrets of the local ice-cream, gelato, and tries some classic Roman breakfast sweet treats. He even gets the chance to train as a gladiator

programme.title: Greatest Hits of the
programme.desc: The stories behind six songs from the decade. Spandau Ballet's Steve Norman remembers how the sound of their biggest hit True was influenced by its idyllic recording setting in the Bahamas. Soul II Soul's Jazzie B looks back on their sound system origins and how their biggest hit Back to Life (However Do You Want Me) blended a bass heavy street vibe with classical strings. Also appraised are Peter Gabriel's Sledgehammer, Simply Red's Holding Back the Years, Frankie Goes to Hollywood's Relax and Whitney Houston's I Wanna Dance with Somebody (Who Loves Me)

programme.title: 13 Moments...
programme.desc: Counting down the fateful episodes that changed the course of her life, this documentary explores the heartache behind the triumphant moments enjoyed by music icon Whitney Houston, together with the fateful events leading up to her untimely death in February 2012. Through a mixture of archive and expert interviews, the film revisits the key moments that changed the course of the entertainer's life and asks why the truly great seem so often to die young

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: A Country Life for Half the Price with Kate Humble
programme.desc: Kate meets a family from Essex who have bought four acres of woodland in Suffolk to escape the growing development and noise pollution of their Rochford home. They plan to develop bird photography and beekeeping businesses, while a vegetable garden offers the opportunity to become completely self-sufficient

programme.title: Britain's Bloody Crown
programme.desc: Historian Dan Jones tells the story of the Wars of the Roses, a 30-year civil war between the House of York and House of Lancaster during which the crown changed hands seven times. Using a combination of documentary and drama, he begins by looking at the reasons for the start of the conflict, revealing it was not caused by scheming Yorkists and belligerent Lancastrians trying to snatch the crown, but by two people fighting to preserve the rule of the ineffective King Henry VI - his smart French wife Margaret of Anjou and his powerful cousin Richard, Duke of York

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of dedicated volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: House Busters

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice visits Essex to offer advice to a Colchester couple unable to snap up their dream home until they can sell their current house. Unfortunately, after four months on the market, there is still no buyer for the three-bedroom detached property - which comes complete with a wild colour scheme

programme.title: The Secret Life of Puppies
programme.desc: Bolt embarks on his first mountain rescue experience with the team, but it remains to be seen whether he has what it takes to be a hero puppy

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.desc: Strange accidents keep happening on the building site and Clockwork Mouse thinks someone is trying to stop them finishing the work

programme.title: Milkshake! Monkey
programme.desc: While preparing a stew, the monkey finds one of the potatoes is growing strange shoots

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: When Merlin the stealth engine comes to Sodor from the mainland for the first time, Percy is determined to see this 'invisible' engine

programme.title: Fireman Sam

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Paw Patrol: Mighty Pups (2019)
programme.desc: Harold Humdinger and his cat, Humphrey touch a radioactive meteor which causes the Paw Patrol to gain powers. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Devan Cohen, Drew Davis and Samuel Faraci

programme.desc: The Brave Bunnies meet Franny the fox and they all go on a mission together to find Bunny Ma's sun hat

programme.title: Little Princess
programme.desc: Algie and Princess find a time capsule, buried by another little princess and her friend many years ago

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: A man tells tall tales to his grandchildren when they ask him questions, but Halifax the rabbit always gives the real answer. In the first edition, Grandpa explains why cats go out at night. Don Warrington stars

programme.title: Floogals
programme.desc: The Floogals must work out what a treasure map is in order to find Boomer's fizzer and get it back

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe meets her biological father for the first time and Rachel helps Joey look a little more sophisticated with the addition of a trendy shoulder bag - which everyone else mistakes for a women's handbag

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler and Monica's relationship finally becomes public knowledge to all but Ross, who is starting to get his life back on track by renting a flat opposite the girls. Comedy, starring Courteney Cox and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross makes a bad impression on his new neighbours, and Monica piles the pressure on Chandler, demanding more commitment than he feels able to give. Joey's latest girlfriend proves a little too fiery - but he is afraid to end their relationship

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey wonders why he has started dreaming about dating Monica, while Phoebe finds a police badge at Central Perk and decides to take the law into her own hands

programme.title: Marley & Me (2008)
programme.desc: Journalist John Grogan celebrates his wedding by getting his wife a golden Labrador puppy, hoping to stave off her desire to have children. Over the course of their marriage, the canine remains a constant companion and never loses his mischievous streak even as he grows from a cute puppy to a 7st dog. Comedy drama based on a newspaper column, starring Owen Wilson, Jennifer Aniston, Kathleen Turner and Alan Arkin

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: In Greater Manchester, Rachel and Chris are being given the run-around by their four dogs, so trainer Graeme Hall attempts to bring the chaotic canines to heel. Cameras also focus on a case in the Midlands, where police officer Simon is being held hostage in his own home by Spaniel Pointer Cross, Dotty

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall trains a German shepherd anxious about her puppy, a bulldog that is always stealing food and faces a particular challenge with a picnic, and a boxer that is driving a wedge between its owners

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Jane goes on a cruise aboard the Viking Sky, visiting Tallinn, St Petersburg, Helsinki and Stockholm, and doing everything from meeting cats to becoming the fifth member of Abba

programme.title: Sally Lindsay's Posh Sleepover
programme.desc: The actress visits Ugbrooke stately home in rural Devon, which has been the seat of Alexander Clifford's family for more than 400 years. Sally meets the live-in husband-and-wife team of butler Alex and chef Tina, as well as the dedicated gamekeepers who help the estate run smoothly. Not one to miss out on a new experience, Sally tries several high-end firsts during her stay, from pigeon for dinner, to shooting clays and dressing up in Alexander's ancestors' armour

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Palaces
programme.desc: A look at the Palace of St James, which originally planned by Henry VIII for his second wife Anne Boleyn, but by the time it was finished seven years later, he was on to wife number four. Plus, stories about George IV and his wife Caroline, Robert the Bruce, and Queen Victoria

programme.title: Princess Anne: The 7 Loves of Her Life
programme.desc: A look at the romantic relationships of the Queen's only daughter, from early partner and Olympic equestrian gold-medallist Richard Meade up to Timothy Lawrence, her husband of 28 years. The programme asks who these men were, but also how the notoriously private Anne emerged relatively unscathed while her siblings and their partners found themselves embroiled in tabloid gossip and scandal

programme.title: Jane McDonald & Friends
programme.desc: The hoat is joined by Blue singer and Hollyoaks star Duncan James (Ryan Knight), singer-songwriter-actress-host and Loose Women regular Michelle Gayle and Britain's Got Talent-winning musical theatre group Collabro for some classic hits and standards, and a chance to enjoy heartwarming stories that celebrate Britain's unsung heroes. As ever, the members of the audience forms an integral part of the proceedings by sharing in the host's secret surprises and participating in the fun

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman (1982)
programme.desc: A Navy recruit undergoing officer training has an uneasy romance with a factory worker. He also attracts the attention of a tough, uncompromising sergeant, who sees his potential and is determined to push him to the limit. Romantic drama, starring Richard Gere, Debra Winger, Louis Gossett Jr, Robert Loggia, David Keith and David Caruso
